hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point jump signals dealers are pricing in real shutdown risk after March 31, likely spooked by hawkish Republican rhetoric on border funding and the incoming spring appropriations deadline. At 56.5%, the market is overweighting political theater and underestimating that both parties eventually cave to the economic pain, making a resolution YES by late March heavily favored despite the current noise.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Jump 10 Points. Markets Smell a Deal.
daily_take.deck: The March 31 deadline just got real, and traders are pricing in a resolution. The 10-point spike to 56.5% suggests the smart money sees Congress cutting a deal before the pain gets ugly. But wait: government shutdowns always look close to ending until they don't. At better than even money, you're betting political dysfunction has an expiration date. History says that's dangerous.

sidebar.headline: CDU's Baden-Württemberg bet just collapsed 27 points. Either the polling shifted or the market overextended. Worth watching for mean reversion.
sidebar.label: CDU Most Seats: 54.9%

sidebar.headline: Atletico Madrid moneyline at 31.5% is a contrarian angle if sharper money thinks the spread is public, stale, and wrong.
sidebar.label: Atletico -1.5: 31.5%

sidebar.headline: Iran Strait closure at 81.5% already prices in an extremely hawkish scenario. Small recent drift suggests traders see less urgency than before.
sidebar.label: Iran Closes Strait: 81.5%
